content:
Hello 
As you can see I’m still anew player with a lot of standard characters and some limited ones 
But I truly don’t know what teams are good for me here, I know I can play counter team and a ghelot team but the counter team wasn’t that great ok it would carry in the early stages but later one it falls and ghelot team im missing Thais and survivability is a problem with it in long battles.

I wan to know what are good teams I can make or I can work to gather characters for, and what realm should I pull for in the realm study banner plus should I stop pulling on the moonless guide banner? I recently lost the 50/50 to pandia and have a guarantee now.

Any good teams for P-dive and the hectic skybound rail?

Thank you in advance and have a great day.

I can clear hard easily with a borrowed pollux(AA) and clementine(AA) but can’t even clear the first boss on madness difficulty. I have unlocked the 1hp for first 5 rounds. Any tips or strategy is much appreciated.
This is what I have. 1 month into playing.

Heyo and welcome aboard : )

Not surprised you can't clear madness after a month. From what I understand the boons you unlock between runs pretty much defines how far you can get.
What team did you use ?

Hello there : )

- Moonless guide : your selection seems fine, I would go after that guarentee indeed

- Did you claim the available codes ?

- Did you read Cheri's handbook ?

- Did you go through the free awakener trials under the record button ( last section ) ?

- Horizon : I highly recommend you stash this and wait until you have a better grasp of how the game works and what gameplay you value most

[AT LEAST 160 FREE STANDARD PULLS]

Go to the thread below for helpful Beginner guides and Redemption codes 

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1509396840330825758/1509396840330825758

Please be sure to check out our <#1304022827824844842>  section! Here are a few resources, tips, and walkthroughs to help you master the game:
## General Guide
- [Morimens New Players Handbook](<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1THjxR9hHs4NWHqSWHHa2dHAGuE_cRGelfzXdBfCnIlM/edit?usp=sharing>) by Cheri
- [Game/Realms/Stats Mechanics Guide](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1511362816362283118) by Mythag University
- [Store Guide](https://discordapp.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1442292634575175710) by Mythag University 
- [Morimens Database and Teambuiler](https://skeydb.com/#/database) by Fjant
## Mode Specific
- [P-Dive Shortest Map Analysis](<https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1483756178340315216>) by Aurora
## Compendium
- [Morimens Compendium (unit builds and other info)](<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TCU7LJRzqKeuvLe97y_TfGZ-j6jq5bZX6hskAAJ2mFQ/edit?gid=167686509#gid=167686509>) by Mythag University
- [Boss Compendium (mechanics and intentions)](https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1489244885025292328) by Mythag University
I suggest you go after Saya and 1 copy of her wheel. Even if you don't like her that much, her corrosion is likely to boost one of your teams significantly.
Same goes for Pontos, so far he seems like a potent support

And then, there's the triple banner.
Pulling for Helot there wouldn't be that useful since you already got her E3 and she's in your moonless.

Pollux, on the other hand, is an efficient damage-dealer for an ultra team and, most importantly, he doesn't compete with other's leap.

Corposant is a fair support overall but will mostly shine if you craft a discard team around her ( and I find she goes pretty well alongside Saya )

Also, if you have cores remaining after you sort your 5 teams, I say you hunt for 1 copy of all wheels you want to use, then go back to the awakeners banner dans pull for enlightenments and limited.
I would not advise looking for the +12 blade for now unless you intend to invest money into the game.

Lastly, GHelot-Aigis is pretty classic and you can throw in Celeste and Sanga for support and durability, or standard Helot for extra strength.

Cheri's handbook contains valuable advices for D-zone, maybe start there and come come with questions if you struggle

I’ve applied the codes long ago 

Yes I did read the handbook it was really  helpful and through it I got a general idea of how a lot of things work 

And what after I get the guarantee moonless guide character? Do I switch or stick to it?

So the plan would be to pull the guarantee then switch to saya’s banner then pontos then one of the triple banner characters?
But wouldn’t the duration of each banner be a hurdle to this approach?
What do you say about getting saya then after that pollux as he’s a strong dps(which I’m in need now) 
Then the other supports I ask your advice on which to pull from them after that?

I thought about but wouldn’t replacing horla out for a mere shielder or healer be beneficial considering her buffs are great?
I thought of team that consists of 
Ghelot aigis helot and a healer or shielded 
Or 
Ghelot aigis horla and healer or shielder 
And since we’re at it would faint be a good healer here or Celeste is better here?
As for the second team would a bluwil team work? Or should I stick to the core of counter and enhance it with other characters that require less involvement?
And what about the standard banner what realm should I stick too?
I’ve asked a lot sorry for troubling you

Horla and Thaïs are very nice. Tawil too but more complexe to handle.
But the thing is, you could go and spend as much as 90 cores to end up with GHelot E4. Nice but not great when you just start.

So yeah, I say you go for that guarantee then switch to Saya/Pontos/Corpo/Pollux then their wheels then dupes.

But that's basically just my opinion.

Sure. If you're short on cores you can even postpone Saya.
And Pontos' banner ends soon but he's more utility than dps so there's a choice to make.

Honestly this triple banner with all great awakeners + new char x2 is really hard to deal with
If you want to go ultra, Cassiah or Tinct or possible options too

About that, I got only one advice : try it all.

A significant part of success in Morimens is doing things because you didn't think it's not viable xD

Plus, you can't really know your teams unless you put them to the test : no matter what advice you get, no one plays the way you do.
Nah, I wouldn't reply if I didn't enjoy it. Am a teach btw 😄

You have a bit of everything so next step would be to loot some banners.

Make some time to read what boons would enlightening each of your favorite awakener bring and, once you got all the WoDs you want hunt these dupes.

Or you could focus on the +12 blade of the giant but imo it's ... not as fun as the gameplay tweaks you get from E3 and OE

Hello there and welcome aboard : )

First off, did you read Cheri's handbook ?

[AT LEAST 160 FREE STANDARD PULLS]

Go to the thread below for helpful Beginner guides and Redemption codes 

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1509396840330825758/1509396840330825758
And yeah, you should probably go for some more pulls. You will eventually want 5 functionnal teams

*But* the first few chapters are rather easy to go through. I say just pick 1 ou 2 awakeners you really like and build a team around them.
It will be flawed but that's okay. This is how you learn.
And when you got 20+ awakeners and tried several linups on D-tide, you can always come back for more specific guidance

2 pieces of advice though :

- Don't hunt wheels duplicates for now. Expanding your roster gives you much more options

- Grab at least 1 copy of Corposant and GHelot (E3 would be best, same for Saya ) as well as their wheels if you can
Think you will manage ?
It is very helpful. Thank you very much.
